\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{import math}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{import numpy as np}

\textcolor{ada_blue}{import matplotlib.pyplot as plt}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{import seaborn as sns}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{from scipy.stats import chi2, norm}

\textcolor{ada_blue}{def head(obj, n=6):}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    if hasattr(obj, 'head'):}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{        return obj.head(n)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    return obj[:n]}

\textcolor{ada_blue}{sns.set_theme(style='whitegrid')}
\end{Verbatim}

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{import numpy as np}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{from math import sqrt}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{from scipy.stats import binom, chi2, chisquare, f, hypergeom, norm, poisson, t}
\end{Verbatim}


\begin{exercise}
\textbf{Before we start}


To run the exercises in this workshop, we need to load a few packages.

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{# Python equivalent setup for plotting and data manipulation}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{sns.set_theme(style='whitegrid')}

\begin{exercise}
\textbf{Customer satisfaction survey}


The client satisfaction results from Table 6.3

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{# Hypothesized population proportions from the base year}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{p = np.array([0.40, 0.16, 0.44])}
\end{Verbatim}

Results from the follow-up study

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{# Observed frequencies from the follow-up study}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{observed = np.array([15, 12, 13])}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{# Sample size}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{n = sum(observed)}
\end{Verbatim}

The expected frequencies are therefore obtained by multiplying these
proportions by the sample size.

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{# Expected frequencies}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{expected = n * p}
\end{Verbatim}

The chi-square test statistic is

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{# Chi-square test statistic (manual calculation)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{chi_sq = sum((observed - expected)**2 / expected)}
\end{Verbatim}

Calculation of the critical value

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{# Degrees of freedom}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{df = len(observed) - 1}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{# Critical value for alpha = 0.05}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{alpha = 0.05}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{critical_value = chi2.ppf(1 - alpha, df=df)}
\end{Verbatim}

Calculation of the p-value

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{p_value = chi2.sf(chi_sq, df=df)}
\end{Verbatim}

Using the built-in test

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{(lambda _obs, _p: chisquare(f_obs=_obs, f_exp=_obs.sum() * (_p / _p.sum())))(np.asarray(observed, dtype=float), np.asarray(p, dtype=float))}
\end{Verbatim}

\subsubsection*{Benford's Law}

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{digits = np.arange(1, 10)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{probabilities = np.log10((digits + 1) / digits)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{benford = pd.DataFrame({'Digit': digits, 'Probability': probabilities})}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4))}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.bar(benford['Digit'], benford['Probability'], color='#00338D', width=0.7)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.set_xlabel('First digit')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.set_ylabel('Probability')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.set_ylim(0, 0.35)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(plt.matplotlib.ticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{plt.show()}
\end{Verbatim}

\subsubsection*{Examples from Benford}

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{df = pd.DataFrame({}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'Digit': [1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9],}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'Rivers': [31.0,16.4,10.7,11.3,7.2,8.6,5.5,4.2,5.1],}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'AmLeague': [32.7,17.6,12.6,9.8,7.4,6.4,4.9,5.6,3.0],}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'CostData': [32.4,18.8,10.1,10.1,9.8,5.5,4.7,5.5,3.1],}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'ReadersDigest': [33.4,18.5,12.4,7.5,7.1,6.5,5.5,4.9,4.2],}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'MolWgt': [26.7,25.2,15.4,10.8,6.7,5.1,4.1,2.8,3.2],}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'Average': [31.2,19.3,12.2,9.9,7.6,6.4,4.9,4.6,3.7],}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{})}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{df_long = df.melt(id_vars=['Digit'], var_name='Series', value_name='Percentage')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{sns.lineplot(data=df_long, x='Digit', y='Percentage', hue='Series', marker='o', ax=ax)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.set_xlabel('First digit')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.set_ylabel('Percentage')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{plt.show()}
\end{Verbatim}

\subsubsection*{Comparison of average with theoretical}

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{if 'probabilities' not in globals():}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    digits = np.arange(1, 10)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    probabilities = np.log10((digits + 1) / digits)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{else:}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    digits = np.arange(1, 10)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{benford = 100 * probabilities}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{average = np.array([31.2, 19.3, 12.2, 9.9, 7.6, 6.4, 4.9, 4.6, 3.7])}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{df = pd.DataFrame({}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'Digit': np.concatenate([digits, digits]),}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'Percentage': np.concatenate([benford, average]),}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'Series': ['Benford'] * 9 + ['Average'] * 9,}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{})}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{sns.lineplot(data=df, x='Digit', y='Percentage', hue='Series', style='Series', marker='o', ax=ax)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.set_xlabel('First digit')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.set_ylabel('Percentage')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{plt.show()}
\end{Verbatim}

\subsection*{Inventory data}

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{if 'probabilities' not in globals():}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    digits = np.arange(1, 10)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    probabilities = np.log10((digits + 1) / digits)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{else:}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    digits = np.arange(1, 10)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{inv_expected = 300 * probabilities}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{inv_observed = np.array([86, 48, 23, 32, 24, 36, 19, 18, 14])}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{df_inv = pd.DataFrame({}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'Digit': np.concatenate([digits, digits]),}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'Frequency': np.concatenate([inv_expected, inv_observed]),}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'Series': ['Expected'] * 9 + ['Observed'] * 9,}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{})}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{sns.lineplot(data=df_inv, x='Digit', y='Frequency', hue='Series', style='Series', marker='o', ax=ax)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.set_xlabel('First digit')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.set_ylabel('Frequency')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{plt.show()}
\end{Verbatim}

Calculation of the test statistic

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{# Expected}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{np.round(inv_expected, 2)}
\end{Verbatim}

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{# Obs - Exp squared}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{np.round((inv_observed - inv_expected)**2, 2)}
\end{Verbatim}

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{# Obs - Exp squared divided by expected}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{np.round(((inv_observed - inv_expected)**2 / inv_expected), 2)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{sum(np.round(((inv_observed - inv_expected)**2 / inv_expected), 2))}
\end{Verbatim}

Critical region

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{df = 8}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{x = np.arange(0, 20.1, 0.1)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{tail_start = 15.51}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{y = chi2.pdf(x, df=df)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.plot(x, y, color='#00338D')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{mask = x >= tail_start}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.fill_between(x[mask], y[mask], color='#00338D', alpha=1.0)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.set_xlabel('x')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{ax.set_ylabel('density')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{plt.show()}
\end{Verbatim}

Using the built-in test

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{(lambda _obs, _p: chisquare(f_obs=_obs, f_exp=_obs.sum() * (_p / _p.sum())))(np.asarray(inv_observed, dtype=float), np.asarray(probabilities, dtype=float))}
\end{Verbatim}

\subsection*{Discrete Uniform Distribution}

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{from IPython.display import display}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{first_digit = pd.DataFrame({}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'digit': np.arange(1, 10),}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'probability': np.log10(1 + 1 / np.arange(1, 10)),}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'position': 'First',}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{})}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{second_digit = pd.DataFrame({}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'digit': np.arange(0, 10),}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'probability': [sum(np.log10(1 + 1 / (10 * np.arange(1, 10) + d))) for d in range(10)],}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'position': 'Second',}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{})}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{third_digit = pd.DataFrame({}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'digit': np.arange(0, 10),}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'probability': [sum(np.log10(1 + 1 / (10 * np.arange(10, 100) + d))) for d in range(10)],}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    'position': 'Third',}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{})}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{benford_data = pd.concat([first_digit, second_digit, third_digit], ignore_index=True)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 1, figsize=(7, 10), sharex=False)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{for idx, position in enumerate(['First', 'Second', 'Third']):}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    subset = benford_data[benford_data['position'] == position]}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    display(axes[idx].plot(subset['digit'], subset['probability'], marker='o'))}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    display(axes[idx].set_title(position))}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    display(axes[idx].set_xlabel('Digit'))}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    display(axes[idx].set_ylabel('Probability'))}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{plt.tight_layout()}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{plt.show()}
\end{Verbatim}


\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\textcolor{ada_blue}{if 'ussteamco_mod_3_residuals' in globals():}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    nres = np.asarray(ussteamco_mod_3_residuals) / np.std(ussteamco_mod_3_residuals)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(10, 8))}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    for ax, bins in zip(axes.flatten(), [6, 7, 8, 9]):}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{        sns.histplot(nres, bins=bins, stat='density', color='#00338D', edgecolor='black', ax=ax)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{        x = np.linspace(np.min(nres), np.max(nres), 200)}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{        ax.plot(x, norm.pdf(x, np.mean(nres), np.std(nres)), color='red')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{        ax.set_title(f'Histogram with {bins} bins')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{        ax.set_xlabel('Standardized Residuals')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{        ax.set_ylabel('Density')}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    plt.tight_layout()}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    plt.show()}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{else:}
\textcolor{ada_blue}{    print('Skipped residual histogram example: source R object ussteamco.mod.3 is not available in Python track.')}
\end{Verbatim}

\end{exercise}
